entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The crop species being studied for genetic diversity and population structure.

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The total number of genetic characteristics in the genetic makeup of a species.

entities.name: POPULATION STRUCTURE
entities.description: The distribution of genetic variation within and among populations of an organism.

entities.name: HYBRID PARENTAL LINES
entities.description: Specific lines used as parents in breeding programs to produce hybrid offspring.

entities.name: DROUGHT-PRONE ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Environments characterized by a lack of sufficient water, to which the parental lines are adapted.

entities.name: SNP MARKERS
entities.description: Single nucleotide polymorphic markers used to identify genetic variation.

entities.name: GBS
entities.description: Genotyping-by-sequencing platform used to generate SNP markers.

entities.name: IDENTITY-BY-STATE
entities.description: A method used to calculate genetic distances between pairs of lines.

entities.name: BAYESIAN MODEL-BASED POPULATION STRATIFICATION
entities.description: A computational method used to differentiate parental lines into groups.

entities.name: NEIGHBOR-JOINING PHYLOGENETIC ANALYSIS
entities.description: A clustering method used to analyze the phylogenetic relationships between lines.

entities.name: PRINCIPAL COORDINATE ANALYSIS
entities.description: A dimensionality reduction method used to visualize population grouping patterns.

entities.name: B-LINES
entities.description: Seed parents used in the production of hybrid pearl millet.

entities.name: R-LINES
entities.description: Pollinators used in the production of hybrid pearl millet.

entities.name: AMOVA
entities.description: Analysis of molecular variance used to quantify genetic variation among and within subpopulations.

entities.name: CHROMOSOME 3
entities.description: A specific chromosome in pearl millet identified as having the highest number of LD regions.

entities.name: LD REGIONS
entities.description: Linkage Disequilibrium regions where alleles are inherited together more often than expected by chance.

entities.name: GENOMIC LD DECAY DISTANCE
entities.description: The distance over which the correlation between alleles at different loci decreases.

entities.name: AGRO-MORPHOLOGICAL TRAITS
entities.description: Physical and growth-related characteristics used to assess genetic diversity.

entities.name: GRAIN QUALITY TRAITS
entities.description: Characteristics related to the nutritional and physical quality of the grain.

entities.name: INBRED LINES
entities.description: Lines produced by repeated self-fertilization, serving as a source of new alleles.

entities.name: ALLELES
entities.description: Alternative forms of a gene that contribute to genetic diversity.
